summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve*, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and the formal description of a new species, *Amphora terroris*. The studies underscore the critical role of morphological data in validating molecular-based phylogenetic placements within this group. Geographically, the research spans diverse global marine and estuarine environments, reflecting the widespread distribution of *Amphora* species.
